info.name: Market Data API
Stock Pricing Data API
Get Bar data for multiple stock symbols

params.name: symbols
params.description: The comma-separated list of stock ticker symbols to query for.

params.name: start
params.description: Filter data equal to or after this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: end
params.description: Filter data equal to or before this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: timeframe
params.description: Timeframe for the aggregation. Values are customizeable, frequently used examples: 1Min, 15Min, 1Hour, 1Day. Limits: 1Min-59Min, 1Hour-23Hour.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of data points to return. Must be in range 1-10000, defaults to 1000.

params.name: page_token
params.description: Pagination token to continue from. The value to pass here is returned in specific requests when more data is available than the request limit allows.

params.name: adjustment
params.description: specifies the corporate action adjustment(s) for bars data

params.name: feed
params.description: Which feed to pull market data from. This is either `iex`, `otc`, or `sip`. `sip` and `otc` are only available to those with a subscription

items.docs:
The Multi Bars API returns aggregate historical data for multiple given ticker symbols over a specified time period.

Returned results are sorted by symbol first then by Bar timestamp. This means that you are likely to see only one symbol in your first response if there are enough Bars for that symbol to hit the limit you requested on that request.

In these situations if you keep requesting again with the next_page_token you will eventually reach the next symbols if any Bars were found for them

params.name: symbols

params.name: feed

items.docs: The Bars API returns aggregate historical data for the requested security. Returns the latest bar data for the queried stock symbols.

params.name: symbols
params.description: The comma-separated list of stock ticker symbols to query for.

params.name: start
params.description: Filter data equal to or after this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: end
params.description: Filter data equal to or before this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of data points to return. Must be in range 1-10000, defaults to 1000.

params.name: feed
params.description: Which feed to pull market data from. This is either `iex`, `otc`, or `sip`. `sip` and `otc` are only available to those with a subscription

items.docs:
The Multi Trades API provides historical trade data for multiple given ticker symbols over a specified time period.

Returned results are sorted by symbol first then by Trade timestamp. This means that you are likely to see only one symbol in your first response if there are enough Trades for that symbol to hit the limit you requested on that request.

In these situations if you keep requesting again with the next_page_token you will eventually reach the next symbols if any Trades were found for

params.name: symbols
params.description: The comma-separated list of stock ticker symbols to query for.

params.name: start
params.description: Filter data equal to or after this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: end
params.description: Filter data equal to or before this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of data points to return. Must be in range 1-10000, defaults to 1000.

params.name: page_token
params.description: Pagination token to continue from. The value to pass here is returned in specific requests when more data is available than the request limit allows.

params.name: feed
params.description: Which feed to pull market data from. This is either `iex`, `otc`, or `sip`. `sip` and `otc` are only available to those with a subscription

items.docs:
The Multi Quotes API provides NBBO quotes for multiple given ticker symbols over a specified time period.

Returned results are sorted by symbol first then by Quote timestamp. This means that you are likely to see only one symbol in your first response if there are enough Quotes for that symbol to hit the limit you requested on that request.

In these situations if you keep requesting again with the next_page_token you will eventually reach the next symbols if any Quotes were found for them.

items.docs: The Snapshot API for multiple tickers provides the latest trade, latest quote, minute bar daily bar and previous daily bar data for the given ticker symbols.

info.name: Get a Snapshot for a stock symbol

params.description: The stock ticker symbol to query for.

params.name: feed
params.description: Which feed to pull market data from. This is either `iex`, `otc`, or `sip`. `sip` and `otc` are only available to those with a subscription

items.docs: The Snapshot API for one ticker provides the latest trade, latest quote, minute bar daily bar and previous daily bar data for a given ticker symbol. This endpoint returns the snapshot for the requested security.

info.name: Get List of supported exchanges

items.docs: Returns a json object representing the exchanges we support. The keys are the short form codes you will see in our responses and the values are their respective full names.

info.name: Get list of Conditions

params.name: type
params.description: either "trade" or "quote"

params.name: tape
params.description: What kind of conditions to retrieve, "A" and "B" return CTS, where "C" will give you UTP

items.docs:
Each feed/exchange uses its own set of codes to identify trade and quote conditions, so the same condition may have a different code depending on the originator of the data.

See [Our documentation](https://alpaca.markets/docs/market-data/#conditions) for more information

info.name: Crypto Pricing Data API
Get Trade data for multiple crypto symbols

params.name: start
params.description: Filter data equal to or after this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: end
params.description: Filter data equal to or before this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: exchanges
params.description: A comma separated list of which crypto exchanges to pull the data from. Alpaca currently supports `ERSX`, `CBSE`, and `FTXU`

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of data points to return. Must be in range 1-10000, defaults to 1000.

params.name: page_token
params.description: Pagination token to continue from. The value to pass here is returned in specific requests when more data is available than the request limit allows.

params.name: symbols
params.description: The comma-separated list of crypto symbols to query for. Note, currently all crypto symbols must be appended with "USD", ie "BTCUSD,ETHUSD" would get both BTC and ETH

items.docs:
The Multi Trades API provides historical trade data for a list of given crypto symbols on a specified date. Returns trades for the queried crypto symbols.

Returned results are sorted by symbol first then by Trade timestamp. This means that you are likely to see only one symbol in your first response if there are enough Trades for that symbol to hit the limit you requested on that request.

In these situations if you keep requesting again with the next_page_token you will eventually reach the ne

params.name: symbols
params.description: The comma-separated list of crypto symbols to query for. Note, currently all crypto symbols must be appended with "USD", ie "BTCUSD,ETHUSD" would get both BTC and ETH

params.name: start
params.description: Filter data equal to or after this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: end
params.description: Filter data equal to or before this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: timeframe

items.docs:
returns aggregate historical data for the requested crypto symbols.

Returned results are sorted by symbol first then by Bar timestamp. This means that you are likely to see only one symbol in your first response if there are enough Bars for that symbol to hit the limit you requested on that request.

In these situations if you keep requesting again with the next_page_token you will eventually reach the next symbols if any Bars were found for them.

Returned results are sorted by symbol first then by Quote timestamp. This means that you are likely to see only one symbol in your first response if there are enough Quotes for that symbol to hit the limit you requested on that request.

In these situations if you keep requesting again with the next_page_token you will eventually reach the next sym

info.name: Get Latest XBBO for multiple crypto symbols

params.name: symbols
params.description: The comma-separated list of crypto symbols to query for. Note, currently all crypto symbols must be appended with "USD", ie "BTCUSD,ETHUSD" would get both BTC and ETH

params.name: exchanges
params.description: A comma separated list of which crypto exchanges to pull the data from. Alpaca currently supports `ERSX`, `CBSE`, and `FTXU`

items.docs: Returns the latest XBBO for a given list crypto symbols that calculates the Best Bid and Offer across multiple exchanges. If exchanges is not specified then only the exchanges that can be traded on Alpaca are included in the calculation.

info.name: Get Latest XBBO for a single crypto symbol

params.name: symbol
params.description: The crypto symbol to query for. Note, currently all crypto symbols must be appended with "USD", ie "BTCUSD" would be how you query for BTC.

params.name: exchanges
params.description: A comma separated list of which crypto exchanges to pull the data from. Alpaca currently supports `ERSX`, `CBSE`, and `FTXU`

items.docs: Returns the XBBO for a crypto symbol that calculates the Best Bid and Offer across multiple exchanges. If exchanges is not specified then only the exchanges that can be traded on Alpaca are included in the calculation.

info.name: Get list of crypto spreads per exchange

items.docs: Get list of crypto spreads for the different exchanges Alpaca supports in basis points.

info.name: News
News API

params.name: start
params.description: Filter data equal to or after this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: end
params.description: Filter data equal to or before this time in RFC-3339 format. Fractions of a second are not accepted.

params.name: symbols
params.description: The comma-separated list of crypto symbols to query for. Note, currently all crypto symbols must be appended with "USD", ie "BTCUSD,ETHUSD" would get both BTC and ETH

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of data points to return. Must be in range 1-10000, defaults to 1000.

params.name: sort
params.description: Sort articles by updated date. Options: DESC, ASC

params.name: include_content
params.description: Boolean indicator to include content for news articles (if available)

params.name: exclude_contentless
params.description: Boolean indicator to exclude news articles that do not contain content

params.name: page_token
params.description: Pagination token to continue from. The value to pass here is returned in specific requests when more data is available than the request limit allows.

items.docs: Returns latest news articles across stocks and crypto. By default returns latest 10 news articles.

info.name: Screener
Get Top Market Movers by Market type

params.name: market_type
params.description: Screen specific market (stocks or crypto)

params.name: top
params.description: Number of top market movers to fetch (gainers and losers). Will return number top for each. By default 10 gainers and 10 losers.

items.docs: Returns top market movers for stocks. By default will return top 5 market gainers and losers.

info.name: Logo
Get Logo for symbol

params.name: crypto_or_stock_symbol
params.description: The crypto or stock symbol to query for. Note, currently all crypto symbols must be appended with "USD", ie "BTCUSD" would be how you query for BTC.

params.name: placeholder
params.description: If true then the api will generate a placeholder image if no logo was found. Defaults to true

items.docs: Returns logo image resource for provided symbol.
